evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential

FAQPage schema has 7 Questions but page shows ~14 FAQ pairs

No TL;DR block (expected

or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')
No visible 'Last updated' / 'Last reviewed' / 'Next review' stamp on page (schema dateModified alone isn't enough for users or AI)
definitional posts should have DefinedTerm schema — not present
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
10 tricolons (3.89/500 words; target ≤2)
Human voice vs committee 3/10 — Reads like a template with repetitive definitional scaffolding; same phrasing recurs across sections.
Genuine opinions vs safe claims 1/10 — Every claim is a safe, hedged generality with no stance.
Unexpected phrasings 4/10 — Mostly predictable marketing phrasing; the 'number a model can steal' line is the only standout.
Specific citations (names/prices/dates) 4/10 — Cites a few engines and one arXiv paper, but no prices, people, dates, or concrete examples.
Point of view vs neutral survey 2/10 — Presents both sides of every question without committing.
Quotable sentence 1/10 — Nothing memorable; all sentences are interchangeable definitions.
Intro earns attention 2/10 — Opens with a definition and buzzword soup, no hook or specificity.

intro_hook 5/10 — The post opens with a definition restatement immediately after the meta description already gave the same definition. There is no tension, surprise, contrarian claim, or concrete stakes established in the first two sentences. The reader has no reason to keep going that they didn't already have from the subheading alone.

specificity 6/10 — The post contains good specificity islands — Profound ($499/mo), Semrush's Jan 2025 launch, the 68% figure, DefinedTerm schema — but several passages, especially in the FAQ and bullet lists, collapse into vague directives ('use monitoring tools,' 'adjust accordingly,' 'benchmark against competitors'). The FAQ answer for 'How do you know if your brand is cited by LLMs?' strips out the concrete tool names already given in the body, leaving only an abstraction. The rewrite above repopulates the concrete details that are already present elsewhere in the post.
